title: buchnera cruciata var. cruciata
intro: Buchnera cruciata var. cruciata, also known as Buchnera or Warata, is a flowering plant species in the family Orobanchaceae, which is a type of hemiparasitic plant that derives some of its nutrients from a host plant. Traditionally, Buchnera has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory agent, antiseptic, and antispasmodic. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Buchnera can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and its herbal preparations are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
